title: STAT+: AI drug developer Insilico Medicine and Lilly ink commercialization deal worth up to $2.75 billion
summary: Lilly has signed a deal with AI drug developer Insilico that’s worth $115 million up front and approximately $2.75 billion in biobucks

title: STAT+: Amid focus on food, FDA leader briefs lawmakers on priorities
summary: Infant formula safety, updating labels, defining ultraprocessed foods, and expanding inspections of processing plants are among the priorities.

title: STAT+: In private meetings, White House works to win pharma companies’ support for drug pricing bill
summary: The White House is working to build support for drug pricing legislation, part of an election year focus on affordability.

title: Otsuka picks up PTSD drug with $700M Transcend buy
summary: The buyout, which includes another $525 million in floated payouts, hands Otsuka a medicine it sees as a possibly “paradigm-shifting therapy” in psychiatry.

title: AstraZeneca lung drug gets ‘surprise’ win in COPD trials
summary: The treatment, tozorakimab, succeeded where other similar therapies failed and could help a broader group of COPD patients than Dupixent.

title: Novartis targets Xolair successor in buyout of startup Excellergy
summary: Worth up to $2 billion, the deal is centered around a drug that could lead to faster and more complete control of allergic conditions than Novartis and Roche’s popular medicine.

title: STAT+: China’s biotech boom is rewriting everything
summary: In today's Readout newsletter, how China's biotech boom is rewriting everything and how an NIH crackdown is disrupting global science collaboration.

title: The impact of NIH restrictions on foreign institutions
summary: The impact of NIH restrictions on foreign institutions, AMA clarifies gender-affirming surgery comment, and more in today's Morning Rounds newsletter.

title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Novo Nordisk boosting consumer focus, FDA approving a gene therapy, and more
summary: Novo Nordisk appointed Poul Weihrauch, CEO of the Mars candy company, as board observer as it tries to boost its consumer focus

title: Rocket gene therapy cleared by FDA for rare immune disorder
summary: Though the therapy, Kresladi, isn’t seen as a big seller, its clearance is a step forward for a company that’s lost most of its value since 2021 amid several setbacks.

title: As political pressure mounts, medical school accreditor drops requirement to teach about health equity
summary: The leading medical school accreditation body in the U.S. has removed language from its standards requiring schools to teach about health inequities

title: Decades after Vietnam War, research links Agent Orange exposure to MDS blood cancer
summary: Agent Orange, the herbicide used in the Vietnam War, had not been linked to MDS blood cancers. A new study does that, and could help veterans get help.

title: STAT+: Trump administration says home care fraud is ‘rampant.’ What do the data show?
summary: The Trump administration claims there's widespread fraud in home-based care, but officials haven't offered any evidence.

title: Opinion: Future physicians need more nutrition education — but not of the MAHA variety
summary: Credible nutrition educators and scientists need to be closely involved as medical schools add more instruction on nutrition.

title: Opinion: Spreading out elective admissions could save lives, strengthen hospitals, and reduce health spending
summary: This intervention improves patient safety, clinician well-being, and hospital finances simultaneously — a rare alignment of incentives in health care.

title: NIH restrictions on foreign research partnerships significantly impacted 1 in 4 U.S. scientists
summary: In a survey of NIH-funded scientists, 25% said their research had been impacted a great deal or a fair amount by a move away from foreign subawards.

title: STAT+: ‘We’re on the inside now’: Meet the man building a political empire behind RFK Jr.
summary: Over the past several years, at every step, health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has been bolstered behind the scenes by a lesser-known name: Tony Lyons.

title: STAT+: FDA approves Rocket gene therapy for rare immune disorder
summary: Kresladi has been tested in just nine patients but has had positive results.

title: First-in-class blood–brain barrier shuttle wins FDA nod for Hunter syndrome
FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Kresladi (marnetegragene autotemcel), the first gene therapy for the treatment of severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I (LAD-I).

title: STAT+: HHS convenes health care execs, motivational speaker on panel to modernize health care
summary: The members will advise Kennedy and Oz on how to lower costs and improve care.

title: Eli Lilly’s deal man, Allogene’s off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck’s Terns buy
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Eli Lilly's deal man, Allogene's off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck's Terns buy.

title: Merck’s deal for Terns sparks debate over a possible biotech bidding war
summary: Some investors and analysts believe the pharma’s $6.7 billion offer is justified given Terns’ recent stock run. Others are convinced a better proposal is coming.

title: STAT+: Denali Therapeutics, Corcept Therapeutics win FDA approvals
summary: In today's Readout Newsletter, Denali and Corcept win FDA approvals, Sarepta eyes a rebound, and Allogene progresses with an off-the-shelf CAR-T.

title: Health influencers talk social media and mental health
summary: In this week's STATus Report, influential health communicators Jessica Knurick, Morgan “Dr. Noc” McSweeney, and Jessica Malaty Rivera chat about the mental health impacts of social media.

title: FDA clears Denali drug in ‘clear step’ for rare disease biotechs
summary: The approval of Denali’s Hunter syndrome treatment, Avlayah, comes after a series of drug rejections and delays that had led to criticism of the FDA’s stance on rare disease therapies.

title: STAT+: A surprise opening for health care reforms
summary: GOP moves to resolve the fight over ICE's funding could prompt more changes to health care

title: Wave crashes on obesity drug update; Kodiak’s reboot pays dividends
summary: Wave’s drug, which is meant to improve body composition, disappointed investors but was defended by analysts. Elsewhere, Kodiak’s revived vision loss treatment succeeded in a second Phase 3 trial.

title: Oral macrocyclic IL-23 inhibitor nabs FDA approval for psoriasis
Maze tumbles despite positive data for kidney disease drug
summary: Investors had reservations about data for a drug that could compete with a candidate from Vertex in APOL1-mediated kidney disease.

title: Beam posts positive data on base editing treatment for AATD
summary: Updated data showed the biotech's approach to the rare lung and liver condition helped restore functional protective proteins in a small study.

title: FDA Approves Drug to Treat Neurologic Manifestations of Hunter Syndrome
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Avlayah (tividenofusp alfa-eknm) to treat certain individuals with Hunter syndrome (Mucopolysaccharidosis type II or MPS II).

title: Sarepta sees early success with RNAi drugs from Arrowhead
summary: Long-awaited results from Phase 1/2 studies lifted Sarepta’s stock and, according to the company, suggest potential for two medicines to treat muscle-weakening conditions.

title: Enhancing CAR-NK cell efficacy in solid tumours
Targeted degradation of amyloid-β
Trikines rewire STAT signalling
Exon skipping to treat Rett syndrome
Wearable technologies in clinical trials for drug development: trends and emerging opportunities
Can a first disease-modifying osteoarthritis drug make it past the FDA?
